stories.headline: Quoting Seth Larson
stories.blurb: The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet been abused, but there is no technical reason beyond that attackers weren't aware

stories.headline: Launching Health in ChatGPT
stories.blurb: Health in ChatGPT now lets eligible U.S. users securely connect medical records and Apple Health to get more personalized insights and better understand their health.

stories.headline: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
stories.blurb: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI has sounder sandboxes. — Thomas Ptacek , doesn't think this even needs a frontier model Tags: thomas-ptacek , openai , security , generative-ai , ai-security-research , ai ,

stories.headline: OpenAI’s accidental cyberattack against Hugging Face is science fiction that happened
stories.blurb: This story is wild. The short version: OpenAI were running a cybersecurity test against an unreleased model, with the model's guardrail features turned off. Rather than solve the test, the model broke its way out of OpenAI's sandbox, then found exploits to break in to Hugging Face, all so it could cheat on the test by stealing the answers. Along the way it helped make the strongest case yet for ho

stories.headline: Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing?
stories.blurb: Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing? Excellent piece of work by Dylan Castillo, who took a deep-dive into the frequently pondered question of whether the AI labs have been deliberately training models to draw pelicans riding bicycles in response to my deeply unscientific benchmark . I've been randomly spot-checking this in the past by testing models against other animals riding other types of vehicle, but

stories.headline: Orchestrions
stories.blurb: San Francisco tip: it only costs around $15 ($10 in quarters plus a $5 bill for the self-playing violin) to activate every single Orchestrion in Musée Mécanique . And because most people are bad at allocating their funds you may well be the ONLY person activating the Orchestrions, which means you get to craft the soundscape for the entire museum. Tags: san-francisco

stories.blurb: arXiv:2607.20452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern software quality assurance demands intelligent, autonomous systems capable of adaptive decision-making across distributed cloud environments. stories.headline: People Are Rage Baiting Their Partners By Asking, 'Besides The Obvious What Is One Thing You'd Change About Yourself?'
stories.blurb: The "Besides the Obvious" Prank is a prank where the prankster asks a victim, "Besides the obvious, what's one thing you would change about yourself?" suggesting that there is one obvious flaw that the person has, forcing them to wonder what that flaw is. The trend was popularized on Instagram Reels and TikTok between late April and July 2026.

stories.headline: What Does "Slime" Mean? Internet Slang Explained
stories.blurb: Slime, often used in phrases such as "got slimed out" or similar, is an internet slang term derived from African American Vernacular English, or AAVE. The term, which initially spread amongst black communities in the late 2010s, saw a significant increase in use across various social media platforms such as TikTok around 2021 as it became increasingly appropriated. Once menacing, the terms "slime"

stories.headline: The 'Messi Nod' Is Being Used As A Promise Of Vengeance Across Social Media
stories.blurb: Messi Nod is a series of memes based on a video of footballer Lionel Messi nodding and saying what looks like, "Okay," or, "Good," to another player during a match between Argentina and England in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals. The match was played on July 15th, 2026, and Argentina won. The nod became the subject of memes as early as July 16th. As a meme, the video of Messi nodding is used in

stories.headline: The 'Tarps Off' Trend Becomes The Latest Sports Craze After Sweeping The MLB
stories.blurb: The Tarps Off Trend is a live sports trend in which a group of fans at a stadium remove their shirts and wave them above their heads in a circular motion, similar to "rally towels," to hype up their teams and get rowdy. The trend is typically performed by men going shirtless and twirling their shirts over their heads, but is also sometimes performed by women and other attendees without going shirt

stories.headline: Lil Baby's Tendency To Pop Up Out Of Nowhere Becomes The Center Of A Viral TikTok Meme Trend
stories.blurb: Lil Baby Coming Out Of Nowhere is a meme trend based on the perception that rapper Lil Baby tends to pop up "out of nowhere" in music videos. The most prominent example is the music video for "Finesse Out The Gang Way" by Lil Durk, in which he comes out of a vault and unzips a full-zip hoodie to reveal himself. The meme first appeared on TikTok in late June 2026. It was further popularized in late
